subject: GRB 200706A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

At 05:32:09 UT on 6 Jul 2020, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 200706A (trigger 615706334.325502 / 200706231).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 89.3, Dec = 54.6 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 05h 57m, 54d 36'), with a statistical uncertainty of 48.5 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 71.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200706231/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn200706231.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200706231/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn200706231.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200706231/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn200706231.gif
